properties.remark: DELAYED REPORT: A PRIVATE WEATHER STATION REPORTED A GUST OF 60MPH NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 33 AND 48. A SMALL COUPLET ON RADAR AND A NEIGHBORING STATION (WHICH GUSTED TO 56MPH) CONFIRM THIS GUST.

PUBLIC REPORT FROM HOME WEATHER STATION OF MEASURED 67 MPH WIND BEFORE LOSS OF DATA. LOCATED IN GREGORY SUBDIVISION, JUST NORTH OF HIGHWAY 412 AND IMMEDIATELY WEST OF THE KINX RADAR. PLAYGROUND EQUIPMENT BLOWN OVER, A MULTI-POINT STAKED TRAMPOLINE BLOWN AWAY, SEVERAL CAR PORTS BLOWN OFF OR DAMAGED, TRAVEL TRAILERS OVERTURNED AND OUTDOOR SMOKERS BLOWN OUT OF YARDS.

UPDATED REPORT FOR REMARKS. MULTIPLE REPORTS AND PHOTOS OF DAMAGE FROM A POSSIBLE TORNADO IN SPRINGDALE, INCLUDING ROOF DAMAGE TO HOMES, STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO AN ELEMENTARY SCHOOL GYMNASIUM, DOWNED POWER POLES AND TREE DAMAGE.

*** 7 INJ *** A TORNADO DESTROYED A COUPLE METAL BUILDING STRUCTURES, SEVERELY DAMAGED MULTIPLE HOMES, BLEW DOWN A CELLULAR PHONE TOWER, SNAPPED LARGE, WOODEN POWER POLES, AND BLEW DOWN NUMEROUS TREES ALONG A 5.2 MILE LONG PATH FROM JOHNSON TO SPRINGDALE.
